title: populus trichocarpa subsp. hastata
intro: Populus trichocarpa subsp. hastata, also known as the black cottonwood, is a subspecies of the black cottonwood tree, native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Populus trichocarpa subsp. hastata include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
